data.title: Scam targets hardware wallet users with fake updates, posing as Ledger and Trezor. Stay cautious!
data.description: Fraudulent paper mailers are designed to resemble popular brands like Ledger and Trezor while promoting fake device updates. The aim is to entice users to visit malicious verification websites. Be cautious of these deceptive tactics and avoid falling victim to potential cyberattacks.

data.title: BostonGene partners with Daiichi Sankyo (TSE: 4568) to enhance drug development using AI analytics for patient selection.
data.description: In Waltham, Massachusetts, BostonGene revealed a collaboration with Daiichi Sankyo, a Tokyo Stock Exchange-listed company, integrating AI into Daiichi Sankyo's antibody drug conjugate (ADC) program. This move aims to enhance patient selection strategies by providing actionable insights beyond typical biomarker analysis. BostonGene's AI platform, known for its tumor and immune biology focus, will drive this integration for accelerated development of ADCs.

data.title: Strategist: Bitcoin bets on Trump, gold on US failure. Gold reflects doubt in economy, while Bitcoin relies on Trump's reforms.
data.description: Wellington-Altus Chief Market Strategist James Thorne suggests that investing in Bitcoin implies confidence in Trump's economic policies, while purchasing gold signifies doubt in America's success. Thorne's argument revolves around the idea that gold is no longer a simple hedge against inflation, but rather a reflection of skepticism towards Trump's ability to address the country's debt crisis. Thorne believes that investing in gold is a signal of the belief that traditional economic policies of printing money and devaluing currency are the only way out of the debt trap. On the other hand, the case for Bitcoin is rooted in the belief that Trump's economic reforms, supported by Treasury Secretary Bessent and Fed nominee Kevin Warsh, will lead to a more productive economy by redirecting capital away from Treasury holdings. Thorne's analysis contrasts these two assets as opposing views on America's future. If successful, the economic reforms proposed by Trump could potentially reduce debt and spur growth.

data.title: Toll Brothers to report Q1 earnings with expected $2.11 EPS and $1.85B revenue. Dividend yield at 0.60% ($1 share yearly).
data.description: Toll Brothers, Inc. (NYSE: TOL) is set to announce first-quarter earnings on Feb. 17. Analysts anticipate earnings per share of $2.11, up from $1.75 in the same period last year. The consensus forecast for quarterly revenue is $1.85 billion compared to $1.86 billion in the previous year. An Oppenheimer analyst recently upgraded the stock to Outperform and increased the price target from $155 to $177 on Feb. 10. Toll Brothers currently offers an annual dividend yield of 0.60%, translating to a quarterly dividend of 25 cents per share ($1.00 annually). Investors are eyeing potential gains from dividends. For those aiming to earn $500 monthly from Toll Brothers, the target is $6,000 annually. Additional details can be found on a financial news website.

data.title: Fusemachines starts 9th year of AI Fellowship Program, admitting 100 fellows from varied backgrounds and areas.
data.description: In New York on February 17, 2026, Fusemachines Inc. unveiled the initiation of the 9th year of its renowned AI Fellowship Program. Recognized as a top provider of enterprise AI solutions, Fusemachines joyfully introduces the incoming group of 100 fellows from various backgrounds and regions into the program. The Company trades under the ticker symbol FUSE on the NASDAQ stock exchange.

data.title: VTAK buys 19.98% stake in AI aviation firm Fly Flyte. Gains access to revenue-generating air mobility sector pre-eVTOL boom.
data.description: Positioned ahead of the anticipated industry-wide commercialization of electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft (eVTOL), a regional air mobility business that generates revenue from its assets is currently experiencing immediate exposure. This strategic positioning in the market is crucial as the industry gears up for future advancements in eVTOL technology.

data.title: Soluna, Blockware partnership grows with 6 MW increase at Dorothy 1 - third expansion, reflecting Blockware's growth.
data.description: Albany, New York - Soluna Holdings, Inc., a company listed on NASDAQ under the symbol SLNH and specializing in green data centers for high-performance computing such as Bitcoin mining and artificial intelligence, revealed an extension to their collaboration with Blockware. The added capacity of six megawatts at Soluna’s Dorothy 1 location in western Texas represents the third expansion in their partnership. This move showcases the ongoing expansion of Blockware's activities and signals further growth opportunities for both companies in the sector.
